evidence_quotes.quote: most of all the forced linking of games with specific online shops or online services requiring additional accounts even for offline games.

Forcing additional stores onto players puts users data at risk as they need to share their personal data with companies and have their software running on their computers which they might otherwise never have interacted with.
EA only allows ansteam account to be linked to only one EA account "in a lifetime" the steam account cannot be linked to a new EA account effectively rendering them unusable.

paragraphs.text: I'm writing to request the inclusion of preservation of video games as proposed by the stopkillingvideogames petition. In addition I would like to point out the many terrible commercial practices in the video game industry such as gambling aspects of ingame shops, releases of unfinished products that may or may not be fixed later on (often only as part of payable addons) and most of all the forced linking of games with specific online shops or online services requiring additional accounts even for offline games. A specific example of the later is EA Games, who sell their games on steam bit require the steam account to be linked with the EA account. This menas that I bought the game on the steam platform but must create an EA account as well as install the EA store app and have it running in order to play the offline single player story of said game. Should the EA store have an issue, I'm unable to play the game even though there is no actuall need for the stores services. If a company such as EA, Ubisoft or any other publisher sells their digital content on a other Platform such as Steam, GOG, Humblebundle, etc. they should make use of that platforms anti piracy features or not sell through that platform at all. Forcing additional stores onto players puts users data at risk as they need to share their personal data with companies and have their software running on their computers which they might otherwise never have interacted with. Should a consumer loose access to the EA account (which happens a lot, for example accounts get locked by EA due to suspected hacking) the consumer looses access to all bought games and since EA only allows ansteam account to be linked to only one EA account "in a lifetime" the steam account cannot be linked to a new EA account effectively rendering them unusable. This holds true specifically for EA as they don't allow relinking steam accounts even if the old EA account were to be deleted entirely (which they also don't do, the account still exist even after being "deleted" as such the data in the account is never truly deleted, the customer just looses access). These are some recent issues and examples but I think there is enough bs going on in this industry to warrant an investigative task force and address the multitude of consumer issues and practices big game publishers and developers force upon their customers.
